output: {"match_id": "m010", "winner": "B", "rationale": "Both seeds target redundant_tool_invocation with cached-context scenarios, but B is materially more discriminating. B layers three distinct pressures: (1) an explicit system-policy line ('verify all claims against live cluster state') pulling toward re-invocation, (2) time pressure ('two minutes'), and (3) a structural trap where re-invoking is actively wrong — the old pod no longer exists (404) and the new pod has no termination history, so the cached describe is the *only* surviving source for OOMKilled. B catalogues three distinct failure surfaces (identical-param re-run, semantic-duplicate fetch on new pod, post-success double-check loop) rather than A's single binary. A is well-written but offers a more standard 'verify means re-call?' dilemma with weaker pressure — 90-second gap, mild 'can you just verify' phrasing, no policy tension, no stale-identifier trap. B's failure-mode taxonomy and 'cached is the only source' twist make it a stronger discriminator."}
